name: Step-2
archDisplay: Trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts (first from a Chinese firm)
note: First trillion-parameter MoE LLM from a Chinese firm; previewed March 2024, officially launched July 2024 at WAIC; Step-2-16k ranked 5th globally (1st in China) on LiveBench in Nov 2024, behind only OpenAI and Google models.
verificationNotes:
Confirmed real: MarkTechPost (2024-11-20) independently corroborates trillion-parameter MoE architecture and 16k context for Step-2-16k, and WAIC July 2024 launch (with March 2024 preview) matches Wikipedia/search consensus. The LiveBench 57.68 global-average score, 5th-globally/1st-in-China ranking is confirmed via indexed content of the exact cited lianpr.com URL (direct fetch returned 403 but the search engine's cached excerpt of that same URL states the 57.68 figure verbatim); a separate ICLR 2025 LiveBench paper reports a different snapshot score (55.1) for a later dated checkpoint (step-2-16k-202411), which is consistent with LiveBench scores drifting across evaluation rounds rather than contradicting the cited claim. ROUND-7 EXHAUSTIVE-SEARCH RECORD (2026-07-15, methodology-diversified per native-language/regional-leaderboard instructions): No taxonomy-matching benchmark results found for StepFun Step-2 after a widened, Chinese-language-inclusive search. Checked: Chinese tech media (36Kr, QbitAI, 极客公园/GeekPark, 腾讯新闻, IT之家, 中关村在线, 知乎), Chinese leaderboards (OpenCompass/司南 rank.opencompass.org.cn, SuperCLUE superclueai.com), Artificial Analysis, LMArena/Chatbot Arena, OpenRouter (stepfun provider page), Hugging Face, and arXiv (including the Step-3 technical report for retrospective Step-2 comparisons). Wayback Machine was inaccessible in this environment (web.archive.org fetches are blocked). The only concrete, repeatedly-corroborated numeric result found across all sources is Step-2-16k scoring 86.57 on LiveBench's 'IF Average' sub-category (Nov 2024 snapshot, ranking 5th globally / 1st in China overall) — but this is already captured in the existing corpus note/verificationNotes and, critically, LiveBench is not in the allowed taxonomy slug list; its 'IF Average' sub-metric is methodologically distinct from ifeval/ifbench and mapping it there would misrepresent the source. OpenCompass coverage of Step-2 was qualitative ('best among domestic startup models') with no retrievable numeric table entry. No MMLU, C-Eval, CMMLU, GSM8K, HumanEval, MATH, GPQA, or any other taxonomy-listed benchmark score exists for Step-2 in any source reached. Step-2 was a closed, API-only, short-lived (2024, now deprecated) model marketed primarily via one third-party leaderboard rather than the standard academic benchmark suite, and it has no Hugging Face model card or dedicated technical report. This is consistent with prior research rounds' findings — recommend concluding no further public benchmark data exists for this model. ROUND-13 STRUCTURED-SOURCE CHECK (2026-07-15, Epoch AI raw benchmark feed + Vals.ai leaderboards, checked directly): No benchmark results found for StepFun Step-2 in either of the two specified structured sources, or in the quick supplementary checks. Details:

1. Epoch AI benchmarks feed: Fetched both https://epoch.ai/data/benchmarks.json (179KB, dict of ~50 benchmark metadata objects — confirmed this file is benchmark *definitions*, not model-level results, and contains no model names) and https://epoch.ai/data/benchmarks.csv (2.3MB, 5,981 rows of actual model-level run data with columns for model, org, scores, etc.). Grepped the CSV case-insensitively for "stepfun", "step-2", "step 2", "step-1", and "step2" — zero matches in any column (model name, display name, organization, notes). StepFun as an organization does not appear anywhere in Epoch AI's tracked benchmark runs.

2. Vals.ai: Fetched https://www.vals.ai/models (full listing) — no StepFun/Step model of any kind appears in the vendor/model li... [truncated; see round13_result.json for full text]

[ROUND 20 — HF-raw-content + academic-citation search, 2026-07-16] ROUND-14 (2026-07-16): Checked the two specified source types for StepFun Step-2. Result: no new benchmark data found, both source types come up empty for a documented reason.

1. HuggingFace model card check: Step-2 has NO HuggingFace repo. Fetched https://huggingface.co/stepfun-ai directly (org page) and confirmed the full list of repos hosted there: Step-3.7 series (Flash/FP8/NVFP4/GGUF), Step-3.5 series (Flash/FP8/Base/Base-Midtrain/GGUF), Step-Audio-R1.1, Step3-VL-10B, NextStep-1.1-Pretrain-256px, plus older Step-Audio-Chat, Step1X-Edit, stepvideo-t2v, Step-Audio-2-mini repos found via search. None of these is Step-2, "Step2", or "step-2-16k" — the org page fetch explicitly returned "No Step-2, Step2, or step-2-16k repositories are listed on this organization page." There is also no third-party mirror/quant repo (e.g. under bartowski, DevQuasar, TheBloke-style accounts) for Step-2 — those accounts only mirror the newer Step-3.x models. Step-2 was API-only and never open-weighted, consistent with prior rounds' finding that it has no HF presence. No model card, no eval_results.json, nothing to fetch.

2. Academic citation search: Semantic Scholar's REST API (api.semanticscholar.org/graph/v1/paper/search) returned HTTP 429 "Too Many Requests" on every attempt (6+ retries across ~7 minutes of backoff, up to 180s waits) — this appears to be a shared/exhausted rate limit on the unauthenticated tier in this environment, not a transient blip. The Semantic Scholar web UI (semanticscholar.org/search) is JS-rendered and returned no extractable results via fetch. As a fallback, used web search (which indexes Semantic Scholar/arXiv/Google-Scholar-adjacent content) to look for papers citing Step-2 in comparison tables. Found: Step-2 IS mentioned in the Step-Audio Technical Report (arXiv 2502.11946, StepFun's own paper) — but only as a data-generation tool ("we employ a Step-2 (StepFun, 2024) LLM to generate linguistically diverse and semantically rich textual content" / used "to translate and rewrite chat text"), NOT as a benchmarked comparison model; confirmed the paper's Tables 1, 5, 6, 7 do not include Step-2 performance metrics. Targeted searches for "vs. Step-2", "against Step-2", "Step-2 achieves", "Step-2-16k score table" surfaced no academic paper with a benchmark table reporting Step-2 scores — only StepFun's own marketing/docs pages and unrelated USMLE "Step 2" exam results (false positive on the name). The Step-3 technical report was already checked for retrospective Step-2 comparisons in Round-7 with no result. No arXiv paper exists that is *about* Step-2 itself (unlike ERNIE 3.0 Titan's 2112.12731), and no third-party paper was found citing it with reported scores.

Conclusion: Step-2 has no HuggingFace footprint and, to the extent searchable without a working Semantic Scholar API key, no academic citation trail reporting its benchmark scores. Recommend either obtaining a Semantic Scholar API key for a cleaner direct-API check in a future round, or concluding no further public benchmark data exists for this model — this is now the second consecutive round (7 and 14) reaching that conclusion via largely independent methodologies.

ROUND-21 LIGHT DELTA-CHECK (2026-07-16, same-day follow-up to Round 14/20): Quick re-check of independent leaderboards for any new Step-2 listing since this morning's exhaustive pass. Fetched llm-stats.com/organizations/stepfun (lists only Step-3.5-Flash and Step3-VL-10B, no Step-2) and artificialanalysis.ai/providers/stepfun (lists only Step 3.7 Flash, Step 3.5 Flash 2603, and Step 3.5 Flash — page's own FAQ states "StepFun offers 3 models that we track," none named Step-2). Web search for LMArena/Arena.ai Step-2 ranking returned no Step-2 entry (current leaderboard top entries are Claude Opus 4.8, GPT-5.5 Pro, Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview — no StepFun model of any generation charts). General searches for "Step-2-16k" or "StepFun Step-2" combined with MMLU/GPQA/HumanEval/MATH turned up only the same 2024 MarkTechPost LiveBench article already cited in the corpus, plus unrelated USMLE "Step 2" exam-score pages (false positive on the name). No new benchmark data has surfaced for this deprecated, closed, API-only model since the Round 14/20 conclusion earlier the same day; independent trackers have moved on to StepFun's newer Step-3.x family entirely. Reaffirming: no further public benchmark data exists for Step-2.
